problem_statement: The document examines how software engineers cognitively disengage from agentic coding assistants (ACAs) during code generation, showing that their oversight of AI-generated code declines as tasks progress and focuses only on output correctness rather than the underlying process.
insufficiency_evidence: "cognitive engagement consistently declines as tasks progress"; participants "allocated minimal cognitive resources to verifying the output and largely neglected reviewing the underlying process"; P4: "I'm not reading all of that"; "none of the participants were able to correctly answer how many functions the generated script had"
solution_mechanisms: slowing down AI decisions to prompt System 2 thinking
delaying AI suggestions until after user analysis
framing ACA as multimodal pair programmer
key_constructs: greedy allocation strategy for cognitive resources
notes: Short CHI workshop formative study with only four participants; primarily HCI/cognition-focused rather than governance-focused.
